title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government has responded to a report outlining standards for artificial intelligence, with measures to address concerns surrounding AI use. This policy aims to ensure AI systems operate in a responsible and fair manner.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education is developing a policy on generative artificial intelligence in education, outlining their stance on its use in the sector. This policy aims to address the growing presence of generative AI in education and its potential impact on teaching and learning.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education, the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, and the Secretary of State for Business and Trade.
summary: This document outlines Ofqual's approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in the qualifications sector. It follows a request for guidance from relevant government departments, likely focusing on responsible AI implementation.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This document assesses the impact of copyright law on the use of copyrighted materials in artificial intelligence development. It explores potential issues and implications of AI systems relying on copyrighted works without proper authorization.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has provided a response to the House of Lords Select Committee's report on Artificial Intelligence in the country, acknowledging potential risks and benefits. This response aims to address and mitigate concerns raised in the 'AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency' report.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: The Committee is reviewing artificial intelligence and its relationship with public standards to understand and potentially update guidelines. This review is guided by a set of terms and frequently asked questions.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The CSPL has submitted information to a parliamentary inquiry examining the regulation of artificial intelligence. The submission is part of an investigation into how AI can be governed effectively.

title: Impact of AI on the regulation of medical products
description: Implementing the Artificial Intelligence (AI) White Paper principles.
summary: This policy aims to address the impact of emerging AI technology on the regulation of medical products. It involves implementing the principles outlined in an AI White Paper to adapt to the changing landscape.

title: Defence Artificial Intelligence Strategy
description: Our vision is that, in terms of AI, we will be the world’s most effective, efficient, trusted and influential Defence organisation for our size.
summary: This policy aims to make the Defence organisation the most effective, efficient, trusted, and influential in the world for its size in the area of artificial intelligence. It seeks to achieve this vision by developing and utilizing AI capabilities in a strategic manner.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: The Transport Artificial Intelligence Action Plan aims to harness the benefits of artificial intelligence in the transport sector. It will do this by working with the sector to maximize opportunities while minimizing potential risks.
